response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of an open-source GIS platform used by Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers. However, that systems work was in DLang, not C++ or Rust; his commercial C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint ending in 2015, and his recent roles center on TypeScript/Node.js product work. He has no routing, tiling, or navigation-guidance experience specifically. Strong engineering craftsmanship and adjacent domain knowledge make him worth a conversation, but he misses the core language requirement for a mid-senior routing-pipeline role.
strengths: Founder-level geospatial experience building and operating an open-source GIS/map-data platform
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching, fibers)
Strong quality culture: TDD, test tooling authorship, CI/CD, and testing workshops
concerns: Modern C++ experience is thin and dated (last commercial C++ role ended in 2015); no Rust
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation-guidance system experience
Recent years focused on web/TypeScript product work rather than systems or data-pipeline engineering
